def move_one_ball(arr):
    """Check if an array of N distinct integers, initially unordered, can be sorted into non-decreasing order by repeatedly applying right shift operations. A right shift operation rotates the array elements to the right by one position, with the last element moving to the beginning. Return True if such a sorted version exists, otherwise False. Note that an empty array is already sorted, so return True in that case.

Consider examples: [3, 4, 5, 1, 2] can be sorted to [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] with two right shifts, hence True.
[3, 5, 4, 1, 2] cannot be sorted by any right shifts, hence False.
